analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet informs that the European Parliament is beginning oversight and debates on the EU-UK Trade and Cooperation Agreement across relevant committees, inviting people to follow live. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Not promoted; no reference to EU history, successes, or learning from past. EU2 (EU is united) – Not promoted; no claim about unity or solidarity among member states. EU3 (EU is useful for its citizens) – Not promoted; while it mentions oversight and debates, it does not assert benefits to citizens or practical outcomes within the EU. EU4 (avant-garde political actor) – Not promoted; no claim of pioneering regulation or leadership. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Not promoted; no mention of rights or values leadership. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Not promoted; although the EU-UK agreement is external, the tweet is procedural and does not frame it as contributing to global peace, cooperation, or well-being. Conclusion: The tweet is neutral/procedural and does not promote any listed narrative.
